metadata.name: Provoke — Goblin Grappler forces Bog Raiders to untap and block
metadata.description: p1 controls Goblin Grappler (1/1, Provoke). p2 controls Bog Raiders (2/2), which starts tapped. p1 attacks p2 with Goblin Grappler. CR 702.39a: Provoke triggers — 'Whenever this creature attacks, you may choose to have target creature defending player controls block this creature this combat if able. If you do, untap that creature.' p1 targets Bog Raiders with the Provoke trigger. Both players pass priority; trigger resolves: Bog Raiders untaps and is required to block Goblin Grappler this combat if able (CR 509.1c: requirement cannot be disobeyed if able). In declare blockers, p2 must block Goblin Grappler with Bog Raiders. Combat damage is simultaneous (CR 510.1): Bog Raiders (power 2) deals 2 damage to Goblin Grappler (toughness 1) — lethal; Goblin Grappler (power 1) deals 1 damage to Bog Raiders (toughness 2) — not lethal. SBA 704.5g: Goblin Grappler dies (damage >= toughness); Bog Raiders survives (1 damage < 2 toughness). p2 takes no player damage since the attacker was blocked.
metadata.generation_notes: Goblin Grappler oracle text (MCP verified): Creature — Goblin, {R}, 1/1, Provoke. CR 702.39a: 'Provoke means: Whenever this creature attacks, you may choose to have target creature defending player controls block this creature this combat if able. If you do, untap that creature.' The Provoke trigger fires at the declare attackers step (when the creature 'attacks'), not at declare blockers like Flanking. p1 chooses to target Bog Raiders (p2's 2/2, which starts tapped). Trigger goes on stack after declare attackers; both players pass priority; trigger resolves: Bog Raiders untaps and now has a requirement to block Goblin Grappler this combat if able. Bog Raiders is a 2/2 with Swampwalk (Available Cards) — no restriction prevents it from blocking Goblin Grappler (Grappler is a Red Goblin, not an Artifact creature, so Swampwalk is irrelevant; Bog Raiders has no restriction keyword like Defender, no tapped status after untapping). CR 509.1c: The number of requirements being obeyed must be maximized — Bog Raiders must block the Grappler since it is able to. In declare blockers, p2 declares Bog Raiders as a blocker for Goblin Grappler. Combat damage (CR 510.1): Goblin Grappler (power 1) assigns 1 damage to Bog Raiders; Bog Raiders (power 2) assigns 2 damage to Goblin Grappler. Damage is simultaneous. SBA 704.5g: Goblin Grappler has toughness 1 and 2 damage marked (2 >= 1) — lethal — destroyed, goes to p1 graveyard. Bog Raiders has toughness 2 and 1 damage marked (1 < 2) — not lethal — survives. p2 life total remains 40 (attacker was blocked). Turn 3 used to ensure no summoning sickness on Goblin Grappler (CR 302.6). Bog Raiders starts tapped to demonstrate the Provoke untap effect.

script.step_note: p1 declares Goblin Grappler as an attacker targeting p2. CR 508.1: Grappler becomes tapped. CR 702.39a: Provoke triggers — 'Whenever this creature attacks' — trigger is placed on the stack. p1 must choose a target for the trigger: Bog Raiders (the defending player's creature).

actions.description: Initial: Goblin Grappler untapped on p1 battlefield; Bog Raiders tapped on p2 battlefield; both players at 40 life; stack empty
actions.note: Goblin Grappler is untapped (no summoning sickness at turn 3, CR 302.6). Bog Raiders is tapped — the Provoke trigger will untap it when it resolves, showing the full Provoke effect.

CR 508.1: p1 declares Goblin Grappler as an attacking creature targeting p2. CR 508.1f: Goblin Grappler becomes tapped as a cost of attacking. CR 702.39a: Provoke triggers when Goblin Grappler attacks — 'Whenever this creature attacks, you may choose to have target creature defending player controls block this creature this combat if able. If you do, untap that creature.' The trigger is placed on the stack. p1 chooses to target Bog Raiders (p2's creature) with the Provoke trigger.

actions.description: After declare_attackers: Goblin Grappler tapped (attacking), Bog Raiders still tapped (not yet untapped), Provoke trigger on the stack
actions.note: CR 508.1f: Goblin Grappler tapped as part of attacking. Bog Raiders is still tapped — the Provoke trigger has not resolved yet. Stack has 1 object: the Provoke triggered ability targeting Bog Raiders.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — the Provoke triggered ability (top of stack) resolves.

resolution.note: CR 702.39a: Provoke trigger resolves — p1 chose to have Bog Raiders block Goblin Grappler this combat if able. As a result, Bog Raiders untaps. Bog Raiders now has a requirement to block Goblin Grappler this combat if able.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Provoke triggered ability resolves. Bog Raiders untaps and is required to block Goblin Grappler this combat if able (CR 509.1c).

actions.description: After Provoke trigger resolves: Bog Raiders is now untapped, stack is empty
actions.note: CR 702.39a: Bog Raiders was untapped by the Provoke trigger resolution. Bog Raiders now has a combat requirement to block Goblin Grappler this combat if able.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority with empty stack — advance to DeclareBlockers step.

script.step_note: p2 must declare Bog Raiders as a blocker for Goblin Grappler. CR 509.1c: Bog Raiders has a Provoke requirement to block Goblin Grappler if able. Bog Raiders is untapped and legally able to block — the requirement must be obeyed. p2 declares Bog Raiders as a blocker.

actions.note: CR 509.1: p2 declares Bog Raiders as a blocker for Goblin Grappler. CR 509.1c: Bog Raiders has a requirement (from Provoke) to block Goblin Grappler this combat if able. Bog Raiders is untapped and has no restrictions preventing it from blocking Goblin Grappler — the requirement is obeyed. The declaration of blockers is legal only if the maximum number of requirements is satisfied; failing to block here would be an illegal blocker declaration. CR 509.1a: Bog Raiders is untapped — legal to block.

actions.description: After declare_blockers: Bog Raiders is blocking Goblin Grappler; both creatures on their respective battlefields; stack empty
actions.note: Bog Raiders is blocking Goblin Grappler as required by Provoke (CR 509.1c). No triggers fire at this stage — Provoke is an attack trigger, not a block trigger. Stack is empty.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority with empty stack — advance to CombatDamage step.

script.step_note: Combat damage is simultaneous (CR 510.1): Goblin Grappler (power 1) assigns 1 damage to Bog Raiders; Bog Raiders (power 2) assigns 2 damage to Goblin Grappler. SBA 704.5g: Goblin Grappler (toughness 1, 2 damage marked) — lethal — destroyed. Bog Raiders (toughness 2, 1 damage marked) — not lethal — survives.

results.description: Goblin Grappler has toughness 1 and 2 damage marked (from Bog Raiders power 2) — 2 >= 1 — lethal damage — destroyed

actions.note: CR 704.5g: Goblin Grappler's toughness is 1 and it received 2 damage from Bog Raiders (power 2) — lethal damage — SBA destroys it, moved to p1's graveyard. Bog Raiders has toughness 2 and received only 1 damage from Goblin Grappler (power 1) — 1 < 2 — not lethal — Bog Raiders survives. SBAs are checked as a batch (CR 704.3).
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority after combat damage and SBAs. Stack is empty — advance to EndOfCombat step.

actions.description: Final state: Goblin Grappler in p1 graveyard (killed by Bog Raiders), Bog Raiders on p2 battlefield (survived with 1 damage), p2 at 40 life (attacker was blocked), stack empty
actions.note: CR 510.1c: Goblin Grappler was blocked — it cannot deal damage to p2; p2 life stays at 40. Goblin Grappler (1/1) received 2 damage from Bog Raiders (power 2) — lethal (2 >= 1) — SBA 704.5g destroyed it, moved to p1 graveyard. Bog Raiders (2/2) received 1 damage from Goblin Grappler (power 1) — not lethal (1 < 2) — Bog Raiders remains on battlefield. The Provoke ability successfully forced Bog Raiders to block by combining the untap effect with the blocking requirement (CR 702.39a, CR 509.1c).
